rows.notes: Top-most line is a large script title. Handwriting is decorative and partly cut off at the left margin; reading 'Arracagia' is uncertain (could be several similar place-names). No amounts present.
Centered heading, clearly legible script 'Redeemed Land Tax'. No amounts on this header row. Vertical ruling lines not needed to interpret this row.
Description reads clearly as 'Hidlington Rectory 1 year'. Amount columns align well with vertical rules; pounds '22', shillings '5', pence '6' are unambiguous and within valid ranges.

Entry text 'Do - Vicarage' (ditto of previous place) is legible but there are no clear numeric figures in the £/s/d columns for this row (small ditto marks appear in the area). I leave amounts blank because numeric ditto marks are ambiguous here.
Description reads clearly. Amount columns well aligned: '28' pounds, '0' shillings, '0' pence. All values unambiguous and valid.
Second ditto for Long Whittenham Vicarage visible as 'Do - Vicarage'. No explicit numeric figures visible (appears to use ditto marks); amounts left blank for clarity.
Description 'Yarnton Rectory 1 year' legible. Amounts read as '14 0 0' with clear alignment; digits unambiguous.
Centered section heading 'Mixed Rents Variable' clearly written. No monetary values on heading.

Description readable as 'Hidlington Augmentation 1 year'. Pounds read as '210' (three digits visible), shillings '10', pence '9'. Alignment with ruling lines slightly less crisp than headings but numbers are within valid ranges. There is some ink faintness around the hundreds digit, hence moderate confidence.
Shown as 'Do. Mills 1 year' (ditto place). Numeric columns are clear: '18' pounds, '18' shillings, '11' pence. All digits legible and within valid ranges.

Description matches earlier 'Long Whittenham Rectory 1 year'. Pounds '115' and shillings '2' are legible. Pence reads as '3' with a small 'q' style mark (interpreted as a farthing = 0.25). The farthing symbol is faint but consistent with the looped q; moderate confidence.

Reads 'South Newington Do 2 years'. Pounds '39' and shillings '0' clear. Pence '10' followed by a clear '3q' style mark interpreted as three farthings (0.75). The fraction symbol is visible and distinct.
Entry 'Do - Tenement 2 years' readable. Pounds '5', shillings '17' clear. Pence '5' with a trailing 'ob' / looped 'd' interpreted as halfpenny (0.5). Fraction symbol reasonably clear.

Description 'Merton Rectory (Land Tax deducted)' readable though the parenthetical is slightly cramped. Pounds read as '240' (three-digit), shillings '2', pence '5'. Ink for the hundreds digit slightly faded but alignment and digits are plausible and valid.
Row marked 'Yarnton Do.' (ditto). Pounds '50' and shillings '5' clear. Pence '5' with a halfpenny loop indicating 0.5; fraction visible though slightly faint.
Description 'Chase Hill 2 years' legible. Pounds '25', shillings '10', pence '7' are clearly written and well aligned with column lines.

Place name is somewhat unclear — appears as 'Olton Ferry' (could be 'Aifton' or similar). Pound and shilling columns read '9' and '7' clearly. Pence shows '0' with a small farthing mark (0.25) — the farthing sign is faint; moderate-lower confidence due to the place-name uncertainty and faint fraction.
Reads 'Bennington 4 years' (handwriting for the place is fairly clear). Amounts '30 0 9 1/2' — pence '9' with halfpenny loop visible (0.5).

Description reads 'Garsington - Blinkard' (surname faint but legible). Figures appear as '9' pounds, '10' shillings, pence '3' with a halfpenny sign (0.5). The halfpenny stroke is lighter than surrounding ink, hence slightly reduced confidence.
Marked as 'Do - Crooke 5 years'. Numeric columns clear: 47 pounds, 5 shillings, 2 pence. No fraction visible; digits align well with ruling lines.

Place-name reads 'Tintinhulle' (variant spellings possible) followed by 'Napier 2 years'. Amounts read '14 0 1 1/2' — pence '1' with halfpenny; the place-name is somewhat uncertain but numeric reading is moderate confidence.
Shown as 'Do - Beaton 2 years'. Pounds '2', shillings '16', pence '1' are legible and column-aligned. Short entry but digits clear.
Reads 'Trethewins 2 years' (old spelling; could be Trethewey/variant). Amounts '34 10 4 1/2' are visible; the halfpenny mark is present but slightly faint, giving moderate confidence.
Clear entry 'Little Tew - Taylor 2 years'. Amounts '16 9 8' are clearly written and aligned with the column guide.

Place looks like 'Warley 2 years' (could be 'Waverley' or similar — handwriting compact). Amounts read '20 17 5 3/4' — pence '5' with three-farthings (0.75). Fraction sign is visible but small, and the place-name is uncertain, so confidence lowered.
Entry shows a surname (appears as 'Kimber 2 years'). Pounds and shillings '19 4' are clear. Pence '8' with a halfpenny sign present (0.5) but fraction mark is faint; moderate confidence.
Entry reads 'Eden 2 years'. Numeric columns '4 4 8' are clear and well-aligned; no fraction marks present.
Place/name is uncertain (reads like 'Givors' or 'Simons'); handwriting here is smaller. Amounts '6 19 3' align with columns but the left-side description uncertainty reduces confidence.

Final visible line reads 'Hawley 2 years' (could be alternate spelling). Amounts '1 19 2 3/4' — pence '2' with three-farthings (0.75). Fraction sign is present but somewhat faint; moderate confidence.
